Name: Conversational map (multi-turn)
Pitch: Remember context across asks: 'restaurants near here', then 'only veg ones', then 'route me there'.
Why USP: Google Maps still treats every query as one-shot. Extends your existing AskMaps panel.

Name: AI trip planner
Pitch: 'Plan a 3-day temple tour from Bangalore' → auto-generated routed itinerary using your saved places.
Why USP: Nobody combines personal-place library + LLM routing well today.

Name: Photo → place extractor
Pitch: Drop a photo, AI guesses location via landmarks/EXIF/visual recognition and pre-fills the Add Place form.
Why USP: Removes 80% of the friction of contributing.

Name: Voice-first hands-free mode
Pitch: Push-to-talk navigation with offline TTS narration; ideal for two-wheelers and trekkers.
Why USP: Google requires data; Apple is iOS-only; nothing serves Indic-language voice well.

Name: AI duplicate/spam moderator
Pitch: LLM-scored auto-flag for duplicate / wrong-category / abusive places before they hit the approval queue.
Why USP: Lets the approval scheduler scale to millions of contributions.

Name: Region summary on draw
Pitch: Draw any polygon → AI summarises 'what's here' (5 hotels, 12 cafes, 2 ATMs, 1 hospital) with one-paragraph vibe description.
Why USP: Natural extension of your PolygonExplorePanel.

Name: Route → travelogue generator
Pitch: Pick any past trip, AI writes a shareable travel blog using your pins, photos, and timestamps.
Why USP: Turns the map into a creator-economy surface; deep moat once users have months of trips.

Name: Natural-language map edits
Pitch: 'Move all my home pins one street north' or 'merge these three duplicate temples' — LLM-mediated bulk edits.
Why USP: Power-user superpower no map app offers.

Name: Deep regional-language UI
Pitch: First-class Kannada, Tulu, Konkani, Marathi, Tamil with localised category names, RTL where needed.
Why USP: Already have atozas-translate; doubling down on under-served Indic languages is a defensible USP vs Google.

Name: Hyperlocal category set
Pitch: Anganwadi, ASHA worker, fair-price shop, gram panchayat office, post office, weekly santhe — categories no global map has.
Why USP: Becomes the de-facto map for rural & government workflows.

Name: Festival / jatre overlay
Pitch: Time-bound markers (only visible during the festival window), recurring yearly with countdowns.
Why USP: Hugely under-served — pilgrims and travellers plan around these.

Name: Curated pilgrimage tour packs
Pitch: Pre-built Char Dham, Ashtavinayak, 7 Tirupathis, Ayyappa, Sufi-circuit packs — one-tap import to your map.
Why USP: Massive existing demand, no clean product owns it.

Name: Local transport layers
Pitch: Auto-rickshaw fare estimator, KSRTC/RTC bus stops with timings, ferry routes, intercity shared-taxi pickups.
Why USP: Solves the 'how do I actually get there' problem better than Google in Tier-2/3 India.

Name: Public utility finder
Pitch: Public toilets, drinking-water taps, free wifi, charging points — community-tagged with photos.
Why USP: Genuinely useful, completely absent from Google. Strong word-of-mouth driver.

Name: Crop / land-use overlay
Pitch: Crowdsourced overlay of cropping pattern, soil type, water table, panchayat boundaries.
Why USP: Powers agri-tech, govt, NGO use-cases — adjacent revenue surface.

Name: Verified ward-level moderators
Pitch: Geo-scoped editor roles with gamified leaderboards; trusted locals approve edits in their pin code.
Why USP: OSM-style distributed trust, but with structured authority maps lack.

Name: Full place provenance trail
Pitch: Public audit log per place: who added, who edited what when, with diff viewer.
Why USP: Wikipedia-grade trust + accountability — competitors hide this.

Name: EXIF-verified photos
Pitch: Auto-reject photos taken >500m from the pin or older than a date threshold.
Why USP: Solves the 'stale photo' problem ratings sites suffer from.

Name: Contribution streaks & badges
Pitch: Daily streaks, lifetime contribution count, rare-place badges, public Wikipedia-style editor profiles.
Why USP: Compounds engagement and crowdsources moderation.

Name: Bounty system
Pitch: Power users post bounties: 'map 20 ATMs in village X', winners get redeemable points / Pro time.
Why USP: Solves the cold-start problem for unmapped regions; novel mechanic in maps.

Name: Place-anchored Q&A threads
Pitch: Ask a question pinned to a specific place ('open early on Sundays?') — local mods answer.
Why USP: Replaces the awful 'Ask the community' feature on competitors.

Name: End-to-end encrypted private places
Pitch: Toggle on a pin to store it as ciphertext — even you (the operator) can't read it.
Why USP: Real differentiator vs Google. Major appeal to journalists, activists, women-safety use cases.

Name: One-line self-hostable deploy
Pitch: docker-compose up ships the full stack including tile server. Pitch as 'your map, your data'.
Why USP: Massive open-source community signal; powers enterprise / govt deals.

Name: Pseudonymous mode
Pitch: Sign up with just a passphrase — no email, no phone. Recovery via printable backup sheet.
Why USP: Removes the biggest barrier in privacy-sensitive markets.

Name: Verifiable no-tracking telemetry
Pitch: Publish an open telemetry config + third-party audit; show a 'no location ever leaves your device' badge.
Why USP: Branding move that compounds — every leak from competitors becomes your marketing.

Name: Export everything + right-to-erase
Pitch: One-tap GeoJSON / KML / CSV / PDF dump; one-tap account+data wipe.
Why USP: GDPR-grade trust at zero cost to you; competitive moat in EU.

Name: Live cursors on shared maps
Pitch: See teammates' cursors and selections in real time, like Figma — using your existing socket.io.
Why USP: Map equivalent of multiplayer Figma; zero direct competition.

Name: Map rooms (Docs for maps)
Pitch: Persistent shared map workspace with chat sidebar, presence, comments, and version history.
Why USP: Strong moat; closest competitor (Felt) is Western-only and paid-only.

Name: Public, embeddable collections
Pitch: Publish 'My favourite cafes in Mangaluru' — followable, shareable, embeddable as iframe.
Why USP: Drives organic growth + creator economy.

Name: Co-edited group itineraries
Pitch: Plan a trip with friends — vote on stops, comment on pins, vote-based reordering.
Why USP: Travel-planning whitespace; competitors are static & ugly.

Name: Map snapshots / permalinks
Pitch: Freeze any map view + overlay state into a shareable URL that always renders identically.
Why USP: Underused growth loop — every share is a marketing surface.

Name: Per-region offline vector tiles
Pitch: Long-press a region → download for offline. Vector tiles keep size tiny.
Why USP: Critical for trekkers, rural users, travellers.

Name: Offline turn-by-turn routing
Pitch: Bundled OSRM/Valhalla; works with zero signal.
Why USP: Google Maps offline routing is degraded; this is a genuine product win.

Name: SMS share fallback
Pitch: Share place / live location via SMS deep link — works on 2G feature phones.
Why USP: Unlocks the half of India that still uses feature phones to receive shares.

Name: Bluetooth/LAN mesh place sync
Pitch: In areas with no internet, sync places peer-to-peer with nearby users.
Why USP: Useful for survey teams, disaster relief, remote villages — virtually unique.

Name: Find My Family / Truck
Pitch: Free multi-device live tracker with privacy windows and battery-aware update intervals.
Why USP: Direct extension of your existing vehicle tracking. Replaces paid fleet apps for small operators.

Name: Geofence rules engine
Pitch: Alert when a vehicle enters/leaves a zone; auto-start sharing only during a trip; auto-stop on arrival.
Why USP: Parents, logistics, courier teams. Higher-margin Pro tier hook.

Name: Driver score from phone sensors
Pitch: Score speed, harsh braking, fuel economy via the existing socket location stream.
Why USP: Selling point for insurance partnerships.

Name: Auto trip diary + tax reports
Pitch: Detects trip start/stop automatically, labels home/work, exports CRA/FBT/IRS-friendly mileage logs.
Why USP: Replaces apps like MileIQ for free.

Name: Expiring live-trip share link
Pitch: Share a one-time link with ETA — auto-expires on arrival, no app install needed for receiver.
Why USP: Beats WhatsApp's 'share live location' UX.

Name: Wheelchair accessibility crowd-tags
Pitch: Per-place flags for ramp, lift, accessible toilet, parking; community-verifiable.
Why USP: Google's AXS Map is half-abandoned; massive under-served audience.

Name: Audio-first map mode
Pitch: Screen-reader-optimised place exploration with spatial-audio cues for nearby items.
Why USP: Genuine breakthrough — no major map ships this.

Name: Senior mode
Pitch: Larger fonts, simplified UI, voice everything, fewer steps to navigation.
Why USP: Capture the rising silver demographic; great word-of-mouth driver.

Name: Low-bandwidth text-first mode
Pitch: Text-only place results on 2G; defer map tiles until tapped.
Why USP: Performance moat in emerging markets.

Name: Embeddable map widgets
Pitch: <script>-tag-embeddable interactive map for any blog / Notion / WordPress / Wix.
Why USP: Every embed is a backlink. Drives SEO and brand recognition.

Name: SEO-indexable place pages
Pitch: Every public place gets a fast static page (place.umnaapp.com/...) with schema.org markup.
Why USP: Free, compounding organic traffic.

Name: @handle creator profiles
Pitch: umnaapp.com/@you — public collections, contribution stats, follower count.
Why USP: Brings social-network growth dynamics to a map app.

Name: Auto QR codes per place
Pitch: Generate scannable QR for posters/menus that opens the live place page.
Why USP: Tiny effort, huge for restaurant/event marketing.

Name: Email/RSS digests
Pitch: Subscribe to 'new restaurants near me' or 'new temples in Kadaba' — weekly digest.
Why USP: Retention loop competitors don't bother with.

Name: Open REST + JS/Python SDK
Pitch: Public, rate-limited, well-documented API + SDKs. Free tier sufficient for hobbyists.
Why USP: Wins developer mindshare. Google Maps API is hated for pricing.

Name: Webhooks
Pitch: Fire when a new place enters my polygon, or a place I follow gets edited.
Why USP: Enables third-party integrations and Zapier-style workflows.

Name: Plugin / extension system
Pitch: Side-panel plugins (VS Code style) — third parties can ship 'Property Prices', 'Crime Stats', 'Bus Times'.
Why USP: Long-term moat through ecosystem effects.

Name: Opt-in OSM sync
Pitch: Push verified place additions back to OpenStreetMap with attribution preserved.
Why USP: Aligns with the open-source ethos; strong PR & community goodwill.

Name: MCP server for AI agents
Pitch: Expose UMNAAPP as an MCP endpoint — Claude / Cursor / ChatGPT can read & write your map.
Why USP: Future-defining positioning; almost no maps service does this yet.

Name: SOS broadcast
Pitch: Long-press → live-share location to 3 emergency contacts for N minutes with a recurring SMS ping.
Why USP: Direct Apple/Samsung-feature parity, but cross-platform & free.

Name: Safe-route preference
Pitch: Routing prefers well-lit, busy, women-safe roads (community-rated). Avoid-zone overlay.
Why USP: Hot-button feature with strong word-of-mouth potential.

Name: Live disaster overlay
Pitch: Crowdsourced flooded roads, blocked routes, shelter locations during storms / floods; auto-expires.
Why USP: Becomes essential infrastructure during disasters; massive earned media.

Name: Nearest 24x7 medical finder
Pitch: One-tap: nearest open pharmacy, ER, blood bank, ambulance dispatcher.
Why USP: Concrete life-saving feature with simple data model.

Name: Personal exploration heatmap
Pitch: Pokemon-Go-style 'territory you've been to' rendered on your account.
Why USP: Highly shareable; powerful retention hook.

Name: Streaks & day-counter
Pitch: Add-a-place streaks, visit-a-new-place streaks, view-a-route streaks.
Why USP: Duolingo-style retention compounding.

Name: Community challenges
Pitch: 'Map 10 ATMs in your panchayat this week' — leaderboard + small rewards.
Why USP: Drives the bounty system + onboarding loop.

Name: Collectible rare-place badges
Pitch: Mountain peaks, ancient temples, historic sites — collect like Pokémon cards.
Why USP: Strong engagement for niches (trekkers, history buffs).

Name: Cycling layer
Pitch: Bike-friendly routes, elevation profile, water stops, bike-shop network.
Why USP: Komoot quality at zero cost — niche but obsessive userbase.

Name: Trekking mode
Pitch: GPX import/export, topo lines, group live-sync on remote trails (works with mesh-sync).
Why USP: Strong adjacency to outdoor + safety features.

Name: Pilgrimage multi-day mode
Pitch: Itinerary with accommodation, prasada timings, queue forecasts, dress code reminders per temple.
Why USP: Massive market, almost entirely unserved by polished software.

Name: Real-estate explorer overlay
Pitch: Overlay land prices, school zones, water table, flood history — crowd-edited.
Why USP: Adjacent monetisation; competitors charge a fortune.

Name: Education map
Pitch: Colleges, hostels, tuition centres, library hours, admissions calendars.
Why USP: High-intent traffic during admission seasons.

Name: Wedding & event venue mode
Pitch: Capacity, parking, catering tie-ups, price band, real photos crowd-sourced.
Why USP: High-value vertical; SMBs will pay to be listed.

Name: No-ads-forever pledge
Pitch: Public commitment + technical enforcement (open client). Pro tier funds the app.
Why USP: Strong brand positioning vs ad-laden competitors.

Name: Pay-once lifetime Pro
Pitch: Single-payment lifetime license tier alongside the monthly subscription.
Why USP: Differentiated against subscription-fatigued users; great launch marketing.

Name: Claim-your-place SMB dashboard
Pitch: Small businesses claim their pin → analytics, photo management, response to Q&A, weekly visit reports.
Why USP: Recurring B2B revenue from a base you already have.
